---
title: "LCA for Social Science Research"
subtitle: "Course Module Index"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: false
    theme: readable
    highlight: tango
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

This course provides a complete, applied introduction to Latent Class Analysis (LCA) using R and the `poLCA` package. The modules follow the full LCA pipeline — from environment setup and research design through estimation, model selection, classification, interpretation, validation, covariate analysis, and reporting — supported by simulated and real data examples.

---

## Modules

| Module | Title | Topic |
|--------|-------|-------|
| [Module 0](Module_00_Setup.html) | R Environment Setup | Package installation, poLCA orientation |
| [Module 1](Module_01_Research_Design.html) | Research Design Foundations | When to use LCA; theory and research questions |
| [Module 2](Module_02_Indicators.html) | Indicator Selection | Variable requirements, measurement quality |
| [Module 3](Module_03_Sampling.html) | Sample Size and Sampling | Power, N requirements, Monte Carlo evidence |
| [Module 4](Module_04_Data_Preparation.html) | Data Preparation | Import, coding, recoding, missing data |
| [Module 5](Module_05_Estimation.html) | Model Estimation | EM algorithm, starting values, convergence |
| [Module 6](Module_06_Selection.html) | Model Enumeration and Selection | BIC, AIC, BLRT, parsimony |
| [Module 7](Module_07_Classification.html) | Classification Quality | Entropy, ALCPP, posterior probabilities |
| [Module 8](Module_08_Interpretation.html) | Profile Interpretation | Labeling classes, visualization |
| [Module 9](Module_09_Validation.html) | Validation | Construct validity, split-sample replication |
| [Module 10](Module_10_Covariates.html) | Covariates and Distal Outcomes | Three-step approach, BCH correction |
| [Module 11](Module_11_Reporting.html) | Reporting Standards | Methods section, tables, figures, checklists |
| [Module 12](Module_12_Extensions.html) | Extensions | LPA, multilevel LCA, longitudinal LCA |
| [Module 13](Module_13_Applied_Lab.html) | Applied Lab | Real data examples: cheating, election surveys |
| [Module 14](Module_14_Capstone.html) | Capstone Exercises | Guided exercises across complete datasets |

---

## Recommended Sequence

Work through the modules in order (0 → 14). Modules 4 through 11 are sequentially dependent: each module loads data and model objects saved by the previous one. Modules 12, 13, and 14 are self-contained and can be read independently, though Module 14 exercises assume familiarity with the full pipeline.

---

## Software

- **R** ≥ 4.0 — [https://cran.r-project.org/](https://cran.r-project.org/)
- **RStudio** (recommended) — [https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)
- Core packages: `poLCA`, `tidyverse`, `knitr`, `kableExtra`, `nnet`, `mice`, `haven`

---

## Reference

Linzer, D. A., & Lewis, J. B. (2011). poLCA: An R package for polytomous variable latent class analysis. *Journal of Statistical Software, 42*(10), 1–29.

---

*Generated with R and rmarkdown.*
